summary: Senior Full-Stack Engineer with 12+ years building production frontend, backend, and cloud systems across Web3 security, marketplaces, B2B SaaS, and agency delivery. Strong match for Lazer's client-embedded model, with hands-on React, TypeScript, Zustand, Redux, Go, Rust, WebSockets, Kafka, Redis, AWS, GCP, Azure, Kubernetes, and Terraform. Shipped complex user-facing systems, real-time data workflows, SDKs, and component standards while mentoring engineers and working directly with clients and stakeholders.

workExperience.title: Software Engineer
description: Co-led OpenZeppelin Defender 2.0 Secure Audit as one of two engineers, building a React and TypeScript module with Zustand so auditors could work with clients, track findings, verify fixes, and deliver final PDF reports.

Owned the EVM side of the Rust-based open-source Relayer, including RPC balancing and failover, cloud KMS signer integrations, chain-specific transaction cost handling, and a fix for a critical nonce race condition.
Built SDK capabilities for developers across Defender and the open-source Relayer so technical teams could integrate the products and create custom automation tooling.
Shaped frontend architecture decisions for OIF and Ascend across rendering strategy, state management, i18n, Kubernetes, and Okta.
Led critical user-flow testing for Defender with Playwright to improve release confidence.
Refactored the TypeScript monorepo build system, reducing local and CI build times from about 15 minutes to 3 minutes while reducing bundle size and speeding up PR cycles.

workExperience.title: Software Engineer
description: Built a live-data presentation feature in React and TypeScript so account managers and clients could use a single shareable URL instead of resending PDF or PPT exports.
Contributed to the REST to GraphQL migration with Apollo across key entities, improving API response times by 35% and lowering server usage.
Helped standardize the UI with Material UI and Storybook for shared component documentation and design handoff, reducing prototyping time from one week to one day.
Improved release reliability by expanding Jest coverage and adding quality gates, reducing release cycles from one week to less than 24 hours.
Built an internal hackathon AI proof of concept using the OpenAI API for natural-language report generation.

workExperience.title: System Architect
description: Improved architecture and delivery for a legacy internal platform, working with product, engineering, and infrastructure teams to reduce delivery cycles from months to about two weeks.
Modernized key parts of the stack by moving from ASP.NET to Angular and from .NET Framework to .NET Core, removing Internet Explorer-only constraints and enabling Chrome compatibility.
Implemented transaction-related API flows with Express.js, including integrations with Transbank for payment processing use cases.
Led a staged Azure hybrid migration across applications, databases, jobs, storage, and networking.

workExperience.title: Software Engineer
description: Implemented real-time catalog and pricing updates with Go and Kafka, moving core logic from a Python ETL flow and contributing to a 21% CSAT increase.
Engineered a Redis conflict-resolution layer that prioritized real-time event processing over scheduled tasks and improved product data reliability by 40%.
Led an internal onboarding platform with Go, Node.js, and Angular, saving $75,000 annually and enabling faster customer-service onboarding.
Built merchant ingestion pipelines from FTP, APIs, SOAP XML feeds, and web scraping for pricing, stock, availability, bundles, and product attributes.
Drove unit testing for critical pricing, offers, and bundles logic with an 80% coverage target, reducing critical rollout defects by 33%.

workExperience.title: Full Stack Developer
description: Delivered five-plus client products across medical, industrial, government, legal, and cultural sectors, working from discovery and requirements through planning and implementation.
Led architecture and engineering standards for TypeScript, React, React Native, and Redux across the company.
Built React Native mobile applications for client products, aligning app architecture and delivery practices with shared web platform standards.
Mentored five developers through pairing sessions, code reviews, and workshops focused on architecture, testing, and code quality.
Worked directly with clients through discovery calls, demos, and status updates, including a short-deadline government project that helped open agency opportunities.

workExperience.title: Fullstack Developer
description: Contributed to building Dxpress from idea to launch as one of Chile's first Uber Eats-style delivery services inside a small product and engineering team.
Built backend capabilities across Python and Go for geolocation and real-time operations, advanced search, and product ETL workflows.
Developed ETL pipelines that ingested and normalized product data from manual uploads, APIs, web scraping, and FTP CSV files, supporting catalogs with more than 5,000 products.

workExperience.title: Junior Software Developer
description: Started as a junior developer and took on growing frontend ownership, helping introduce Node.js and AngularJS into the agency stack.

education.degree: Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science

company_profile.description: Lazer is a digital product studio that designs, engineers, and grows products for startups, venture-backed companies, and enterprises.
company_profile.mission: Lazer says it started to help create some of the world's most important and impactful products.
company_profile.vision: Inferred from official pages: keep building high-impact digital products across startup and enterprise environments with senior product, design, and engineering teams.
services: Full-stack web and mobile app development
Web3 infrastructure, DeFi, and trading interfaces
company_profile.industry: Digital product consulting, product engineering, AI, commerce, crypto, Web3, fintech, SaaS, healthcare, and mobile.
company_profile.business_model: B2B product design and engineering services with embedded senior teams, project delivery, and productized accelerators.
recent_signals: Official site says Lazer has worked with 350+ clients, including early-stage startups and Fortune 100 or Fortune 500 enterprises.
The Web3 page says Lazer works with 70+ Web3 enterprises and startups and offers DeFi and trading interface development.
The clients page lists Polymarket, Uniswap, Coinbase, Goldfinch, Berachain, XMTP, Lightspark, Azura, Alchemy, and Cozy Finance among Web3 or fintech clients.
The AI page presents embedded senior pods, AI infrastructure, design, development, product strategy, and forward deployed engineering services.
Official insights list Agentic Commerce Accelerator and CommerceBench as 2026 commerce and AI initiatives.
